calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Gulick for and with Teresa Welborn.

Mr. Gulick stated that he got some of the issues raised by the Examiner taken care of, that Rosalyn Gains faxed a letter indicating that she wants not part of the residue, that he filed receipts for 83 out 91 specific pecuniaries (indivicuals and entities) and that calculation of the residue is correct. The Court stated that the Third Amendment was not served on everybody. Mr. Gulick proposed giving them (McRenner and Parina) more notice and also stated that he can obtain consents or waivers of notice. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Continued To Sep-24-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Gulick for and with Teresa Welborn.

Mr. Gulick provided an endorsed copy of a declaration that was filed today which addresses and acknowledges the error made with regard to the Planned Parenthood gift and attaches a copy of an additional page of the address book. The green address book was also provided in open Court for the Judge to review (green adress book returned to Petitioner in open Court). The Court granted the accounting petition in part with respect to those Parties who are to receive the pecuniary bequests. The remaining balance of the accounting petition with respect to the seven Parties for whom Counsel is now requesting that they not be paid was continued to September 24, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file an amended petition and to provide notice to those seven individuals. The amended petition is to have the same hearing date of September 24, 2012. Counsel is to provide a revised proposed order reflecting the above.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2012 Continued To Jul-23-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Gulick for Teresa Welborn.

Mr. Gulick stated that the declarations describing the efforts made in locating the Parties who witnessed the will were filed on Friday and provided endorsed copies to the Court. The declarations received were from Eileen Tianco (U.S. Bank employee), Teresa Welborn and Mr. Gulick. The Court granted the petition and admitted the will (signing the order for the same in open court). Bond in the amount of $810,000.00 is required. The Court informed Mr. Gulick that he will need to file a petition under Probate code 11700 to clarify ambiguities in the documents and determine the validty and intent of the handwritten interlineations.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5220. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Continued To Jun-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Gulick for and with Teresa Welborn.

The Court stated that there was an issue with the will and the letter of instruction, that the will had no attestation clause and that the will was not self proving. Mr. Gulick stated that an Employee at the Bank stated that they maybe recognized one of the Employee's signature (witness) but that the Bank would not provide any information on that Employee's where abouts, and that they may have to do discovery and issue a subpeona to the Bank. Mr. Gulick will submit an ex parte petition for drop off requesting an order allowing discovery along with a declaration from Teresa Welborn with an exhibit of the Brother's death certificate. The matter was continued to June 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for hearing on the amended petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Continued To Apr-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathleen Hunt for and with Teresa Welborn.

Ms. Hunt provided an endorsed filed copy of the Proof of Service filed this morning, and stated that she would file a letter explaining the letter of instruction and the date of death of the Decedent's Brother. The Court pointed out that the Proof of Service did not indicate notice of the amended petition. The matter was continued to April 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Clauzel.
